content: Hey Saturday, I just had an appointment with my physiotherapist, Dr. Elena Santos, about my lower back pain. It’s been around a 6/10 lately and gets worse after sitting all day.
Thanks for sharing that—6/10 is significant, especially if prolonged sitting triggers it. What plan did Dr. Elena Santos recommend so we can help you stick to it consistently?
She wants me doing core work 3 times a week: bird-dog, dead bug, and glute bridges. She also said my desk setup is probably part of the issue.

That’s a solid plan—those exercises are great for spinal stability without overloading your back. For your desk, start with your monitor at eye level and add proper lumbar support so your lower back isn’t collapsing during long sitting blocks.
Yeah, I’m going to raise my monitor with a stand and use a lumbar cushion in my chair. She also scheduled a progress check in 6 weeks to see if pain and mobility improve.
Perfect, that gives you a clear timeline and measurable goal. If you do the routine 3x/week and keep the ergonomic changes consistent, you should have useful progress data by that 6-week follow-up.
Can you help me keep it simple? I want to actually follow through and hopefully get the pain down from 6/10 before I see Dr. Santos again.

Absolutely—keep it to a checklist: core routine on 3 set days each week, monitor at eye level daily, and lumbar support whenever you sit. Track pain once each evening (0–10) so you can show Dr. Santos a clear trend at your 6-week check.
